## [06:03 - 09:07]

And I've learned over the years that if you truly want to make an offer work, if you want to make a convert, especially on cold traffic, the upsell has to be planned out and thoroughly thought of before you even create anything. So I'll give you an example. Tim and Dan who are in this group, I gave them some feedback a couple of weeks ago on one of their fitness watches that they're doing as a free post shipping offer. And I said one of the while the offer was doing really well, one of the issues with it is it didn't really have a natural upsell for it. It's a great front end offer, but it's tough. We haven't really been able to think of a natural upsell for it. Compare that to something like supplements, which I know if you buy one bottle of this supplement or three bottles of this supplement, I know I can upsell you three more of that same supplement. That's an offer that's proven to work. I know it works. It's a natural fit. So step one is really, like I said, before you even put the product out, figuring out is there a natural upsell that I can do? Is there something? I want to make sure I got the chat up here. By the way, feel free to, if you guys got any questions, we'll all explain this, drop something in the chat and let me know. So yeah, just don't put out an offer and say, okay, now what can we upsell? I would even go as far to say if a product does not have a good natural upsell, I would not even create that product, even if it is a good product. Because like I said, there's three components of making an offer work, and that's the sales page and the upsells. And if you can't get the upsells to work, you're never really going to get the offer to work. So let's dig into some examples of what I consider good natural upsells. So there's three basic ones that always work. There's the ones I always try to focus on. Number one is more of the same. I'll explain these more in depth with some examples, more of the same. So like that goes back to if they bought supplements, we'll sell more of that same supplement. Number two is do the work for them. So if they bought your course on Facebook ads, your first upsell being copy and paste kind of Facebook ads that they can just upload that will work and help them get leads or clients or whatever it is. Number three is get them results faster. So they're buying a diet program. If they're buying a how to make money thing, if there's a way that you can get them results faster and faster, that always works.

## [09:07 - 12:07]

So in terms of the hook of the upsell, these are the three that always work. So if your upsell does not fit into one of these three, I would have some serious reservations about it working. Now, I'll dig into some of the examples here. So an example of more of the same. So my old company, Patriot Health Alliance, we had a sister company called FourPatriots, which I didn't own any part of, but I was very, I kind of knew all the stuff that was going on there. One of their writers Amy, she's on this call. So if they buy a survival food three-day package from them, the first upsell is the three-month package. So you're literally getting this little three-day package. Now we're going to try to save this big old bucket of survival food, which is a three-month package. That's literally a super easy example of more of the same. It offers like that always work. Rolling down here, you'll see very similar ones. So supplements do the exact same thing. They buy three bottles of a supplement, you upsell them, three more bottles of the same supplement. Sometimes at a discount, sometimes with extra bonuses, sometimes with some type of scarcity play where we only have so many left or stuck up now because we run out, whatever the angle is, it's still the same. You're selling them more of the same. Another example of this, this is how Agora's entire business is built, which is newsletter subscriptions. So people subscribe to the newsletter for one year or two year, which is paid up front for like, usually like 49 bucks or 79 bucks, something like that. And their main upsell on pretty much every offer they put out is upgrading them from the one year subscription that they bought to a lifetime subscription. So you literally just bought a subscription to a newsletter for X amount of time. We're going to upgrade you to the lifetime one for, I don't know the prices, usually I think it's like $200 or something like that. And they'll throw in like bonuses and all kinds of stuff to entice you to upgrade. But that's in a digital format, the exact same as what we do to supplement, what we do to survival food, it's literally more of the same. Does this make sense to everybody? Cool. So let's keep going. So books, they buy the main book, let's say you're selling a free plus shipping offer or you're selling a book for 15 bucks or whatever. They buy the main book and then you upsell them like the advanced masterclass. And the advanced masterclass has secrets that are not in the book. So you see this a lot in the IM space, you see this a lot in like get rich, like in real estate type stuff.

## [1:00:46 - 1:03:46]

So we'll keep moving. So one thing I want to, uh, talk about here is that in the first 30 seconds of your upsell, one of the biggest mistakes people make is literally just going into pitching the upsell. So you really want to acknowledge the purchase they just made. You want to tell them they're in the right spot and you want to tell them something about like their orders getting ready or they'll have access to the order or whatever the case is. Cause you have to think, especially in a lot of our markets, you have people buying this that are 50, 60, 70 years old. Um, they assume when they buy this that they should be landing on like a confirmation page, like with Amazon. So if you just send them to an upsell and all of a sudden you just start talking and like pitch them on something else, they get a ton of anxiety going. They have no idea what's going on. Uh, they think like something's broken. They think they clicked on the wrong link. So it's your job to really calm that anxiety and make sure that they know that everything's okay. They're in the right place. I'm going to show you an example of this. Um, but that, that's a big, big mistake. I say a lot of people, even like really smart marketers making. Um, so a couple of last things before we show some of the examples, once you have a format that works, uh, replicated over and over again. So Agora is without a doubt the masters at this. They know from selling a book as a premium with a newsletter subscription to the first upsell being the lifetime newsletter subscription is just like a home run and all they got to do is just keep pumping out products that like fit that mold. Um, once you have a mold that works and you know that it works, just keep coming up with products that kind of fit that mold, because it's really hard to just start with new, like if you're really good at doing supplements, uh, or let's say you're really good at going from an ebook to upselling supplements, keep putting out offers like that, because figuring out how to make the funnel work is really freaking hard. But if you've figured it out once or twice, like you'd be really dumb to not keep pumping out offers that are very similar to that because you already know the model and you know what works.
